column1: # text = 28-Year-Old Chef Found Dead at San Francisco Mall A 28-year-old chef who had recently moved to San Francisco was found dead in the stairwell of a local mall this week.
# text = But the victim's brother says he can't think of anyone who would want to hurt him, saying, \"Things were finally going well for him.\"
# text = The body found at the Westfield Mall Wednesday morning was identified as 28-year-old San Francisco resident Frank Galicia, the San Francisco Medical Examiner's Office said.
# text = The San Francisco Police Department said the death was ruled a homicide and an investigation is ongoing.

# text = The victim's brother, Louis Galicia, told ABC station KGO in San Francisco that Frank, previously a line cook in Boston, had landed his dream job as line chef at San Francisco's Sons & Daughters restaurant six months ago.
# text = A spokesperson for Sons & Daughters said they were "shocked and devastated" by his death.
# text = \"We are a small team that operates like a close knit family and he will be dearly missed,\" the spokesperson said.
# text = Our thoughts and condolences are with Frank's family and friends at this difficult time.
# text = Louis Galicia said Frank initially stayed in hostels, but recently, \"Things were finally going well for him.\"
# text = \"He found an apartment, he was dating a girl,\" Louis Galicia told KGO.
# text = Louis Galicia said he could not think of anyone who would want to hurt his younger brother.
# text = He was a kind spirit with a big heart.
# text = His way of connecting with the family was always making us a dish, making us dinner," Louis Galicia said.
# text = He never wanted to be in any kind of altercation.
# text = He was the brother that went with the flow.
# text = "With everything else that's going wrong with the world, he was that diamond in the rough that was shining bright every day," he said.
# text = Anyone with information is asked to call the SFPD Tip Line at 415-575-4444.
